title: Objects, Scope, Linkage, Storage Duration, and Lifetime · C Contract Brief: Const Volatile Limits
summary: An embedded controller caches addresses across callbacks while automatic, static, thread, and allocated objects begin and end at different times. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem const volatile limits increment in object lifetime and address ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use const for mutation contracts and volatile only for its defined access semantics, never as ownership or synchronization.
Treat a reused address as a new lifetime and preserve generation or identity evidence where stale handles are possible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
